directive: In src/jobs/reconciliation/diffInventory.ts aliasMatch(): when a warehouse SKU resolves to multiple DB SKUs via the reverse-alias index, sum dbQty across all aliased DB SKUs and compare that sum to warehouseQty to compute delta; emit a single {sku, warehouseQty, dbQty: <sum>, delta} record (do not emit one per alias, do not compare per-alias). Update the 'one warehouse SKU → multiple DB SKUs' test to assert non-flagging when the alias sum equals warehouseQty (e.g., SKU-W=10, SKU-A=7, SKU-B=3 → no discrepancy), and add a companion case where the sum differs (e.g., SKU-W=10, SKU-A=7, SKU-B=2 → delta=1).
rationale: Reviewer's Round 2 rejection is a concrete, actionable logic defect, not a spec defect. The phase spec permits one warehouse SKU mapping to multiple DB SKUs but did not pin down aggregation semantics; the Reviewer has now articulated the correct semantics (sum dbQty across aliases before comparing to warehouseQty). The fix is localized to aliasMatch aggregation and one test. This is a quality concern the Executor can resolve with a specific directive — neither a plan defect nor scope expansion. Non-goals (read-only flagging, ingestion untouched, 60s timeout) are unaffected.
